content.verdict: Amankora Gangtey is the most dramatically sited of all the Aman lodges in Bhutan — a glass-walled pavilion overlooking the remote Phobjikha valley, where black-necked cranes winter on the floor of a U-shaped glacial bowl. Just 8 suites and the longest road journey of any Aman make this the deep-Himalaya lodge for honeymooners who want true wilderness with their luxury.
content.best_room: All 8 suites are identical, each with a bukhari fireplace, soaking tub, and floor-to-ceiling glass facing the valley. There is no upgrade — the lodge itself is the splurge. Request a suite at either end of the row for the most privacy.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Arrival in Phobjikha valley
itinerary_7_nights.description: Long scenic drive from Punakha or Thimphu, climbing through rhododendron forest to the 3,000m Phobjikha valley. Welcome ara by the central fire, in-suite hot stone bath, dinner with full valley views.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Gangtey Goemba monastery
itinerary_7_nights.description: Morning visit to the 17th-century Gangtey Goemba monastery, the most important Nyingma institution in western Bhutan. Sit in on the morning chant. Afternoon nature walk on the Gangtey loop trail across the valley floor.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Black-necked crane center
itinerary_7_nights.description: Visit the Royal Society for Protection of Nature crane center — November-March is when 300+ cranes winter here from Tibet. Afternoon picnic at a viewpoint, sunset back at the lodge fire.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Drive back to Punakha
itinerary_7_nights.description: Cross back over Lawala Pass to the warmer Punakha valley. Visit Chimi Lhakhang, the Divine Madman's fertility temple, on the way. Evening transfer to Amankora Punakha or onward.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Punakha Dzong day
itinerary_7_nights.description: If staying multi-lodge: tour Punakha Dzong, raft the Mo Chhu, picnic at Khamsum Yulley Namgyal Chorten.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Spa and stillness
itinerary_7_nights.description: Couples massage, hot stone bath, traditional textile demonstration. Evening reading by the bukhari.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Departure
itinerary_7_nights.description: Drive back to Paro for the early-morning Druk Air departure, with stops at Dochula Pass and the 108 chortens.

caveats: Phobjikha sits at 3,000m — arrive via Punakha or Thimphu rather than direct from Paro to acclimatize.
Black-necked cranes only winter here November-March — visit in those months for the signature wildlife experience.
The drive from Punakha is 3-4 hours on winding mountain roads; bring motion-sickness remedies if you're sensitive.
Sustainable Development Fee of $200 per person per night applies, and the Aman tour-operator visa takes 4-6 weeks.

faqs.question: Is Amankora Gangtey good for honeymooners?
faqs.answer: Yes — for couples who want wilderness and silence as their backdrop. The 8-suite scale, glass-walled lodge, and remote valley setting create a meditative, romantic atmosphere unlike anywhere else in Bhutan.

faqs.question: When is the best time to visit?
faqs.answer: November-February for the black-necked cranes and crisp blue skies. March-May for rhododendrons. Avoid monsoon June-August for the Phobjikha drive.

faqs.question: How far in advance should we book?
faqs.answer: Six months — with only 8 suites, Gangtey is one of the hardest lodges to secure, especially crane season.

faqs.question: Do we need a guide?
faqs.answer: Yes, mandatory under Bhutanese tourism rules. Aman provides a licensed guide and driver throughout your stay.

faqs.question: What's the best room type?
faqs.answer: All 8 suites are identical. Request an end-of-row suite for the most quiet.

faqs.question: How do we get to Amankora Gangtey?
faqs.answer: Fly to Paro, then drive 5-6 hours via Thimphu and Punakha, or break the journey overnight at Amankora Punakha. Aman includes all transfers.
